title: Budget governor: hard per-cycle caps (tokens/USD/sources/wall-clock), fail-closed stop
context: The 'perpetual loop' is only safe because every cycle is bounded by a hard budget cap; Elyos rules forbid uncapped or headless autonomous spend. This safety-critical M0 subsystem meters every LLM/fetch call against the remaining per-cycle budget and stops fail-closed when a cap would be exceeded, with no in-cycle override path. A bypass would break the budget refusal, so it is high risk-tier and gate-reviewed.
objective: Implement the budget governor enforcing hard per-cycle caps on tokens, USD, source count, and wall-clock, metering every LLM/fetch call and refusing the call that would exceed the cap, stopping the cycle fail-closed with partial work persisted and a truncation note to the ledger, with no in-cycle override.
acceptanceCriteria: Enforces per-cycle caps on tokens, USD, source count, and wall-clock; meters every LLM/fetch call against remaining budget and refuses the call that would exceed it
On cap trip, the cycle stops fail-closed with partial work persisted and a truncation note written to the ledger; a cap-trip test proves the stop occurs and nothing runs past the cap
No in-cycle override path; raising a cap is a between-cycle, governance-logged human action
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer (recorded)
